to prepare derivative works of the Software

conditions.commentary: Notable carve-out: the include-copyright/include-license obligation does not apply when the distribution consists solely of machine-executable object code generated by a source language processor. So binary-only redistributions are exempt from the notice requirement, which is a clear difference from MIT.

citations.note: The copyright notices in the Software and this entire statement ... must be included in all copies of the Software ... and all derivative works of the Software, unless such copies or derivative works are solely in the form of machine-executable object code

conditions.commentary: Attribution is required for source redistributions but waived for binary-only distributions per the object-code carve-out at the end of the condition.

citations.note: IN NO EVENT SHALL THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS OR ANYONE DISTRIBUTING THE SOFTWARE BE LIABLE FOR ANY DAMAGES OR OTHER LIABILITY
